hypotheses: The 'pm' output in TopModule is not toggling correctly at the 12-hour rollover, causing persistent mismatches with the reference model [evidence: log_excerpt L10 'Output 'pm' has 177947 mismatches. First mismatch occurred at time 38270.']

The logic for incrementing and toggling 'pm' in the hour rollover case may be incorrect or incomplete, possibly due to an off-by-one error or mishandling of the 12->1 transition [evidence: RTL L42-L46 'if ((hour_tens == 4'd1) && (hour_ones == 4'd2)) ... pm <= ~pm;']

The rest of the outputs (hh, mm, ss) match the reference, indicating the issue is isolated to the 'pm' logic and not a global state or reset problem [evidence: log_excerpt L11-L13 'Output 'hh' has no mismatches.', etc.]
likely_failure_points: Hour increment and PM toggle logic in the always @(posedge clk) block, specifically lines 42-46 [evidence: RTL L42-L46]
Potential missing or incorrect handling of the AM/PM toggle when rolling over from 11:59:59 to 12:00:00 [evidence: RTL L42-L46, testbench sequence at L113-L117 'PM roll-over']
recommended_probes: Add a probe to monitor the values of hour_tens, hour_ones, and pm at every hour rollover, especially at 11:59:59 -> 12:00:00 and 12:59:59 -> 1:00:00
Add a probe to compare pm_dut and pm_ref at every clock edge, and log the time and values when they diverge
Instrument the testbench to print the values of all internal hour/minute/second registers and pm at the first mismatch time (38270 ps)
analysis_notes: The evidence is strong that the failure is isolated to the 'pm' output, with 177947 mismatches starting at time 38270, while all other outputs match the reference. The RTL code for hour increment and PM toggle is the only place where 'pm' is assigned outside of reset, and the logic appears to only toggle 'pm' when rolling over from 12 to 1. If the reference expects a toggle at a different point (e.g., at 11:59:59 -> 12:00:00 rather than at 12:59:59 -> 1:00:00), this could explain the persistent mismatch. The testbench includes a 'PM roll-over' scenario, but without more detailed signal traces, the exact transition point cannot be confirmed. No evidence of reset or enable issues is present. Additional probes at the hour and PM transitions will quickly falsify or confirm this hypothesis.
